b: HSA: requires HDHP; employee + employer funded; funds roll over; portable; triple tax benefit. FSA: employer plan; use-it-or-lose-it (grace period or $660 carryover option); not portable. HRA: employer-funded only; not portable but rollover allowed by employer.

Funded pre-tax. Use-it-or-lose-it rule: must use funds or forfeit. Employers may offer a grace period (2.5 months) OR allow up to $660 carryover (2025) (not both). Eligible for medical AND dependent care expenses.
Reliance on others for financial support rather than self-sufficiency. The dependent person often fears that the supply of money will stop and may lose financial autonomy.
